what_it_does: Defines render-independent FACE-1 surface archetypes by naming each surface, its structural slots, expected data bindings, organism/composition type, and responsive intent while deferring visual treatment and interaction states.
observations: The file frames FACE-1 as six surfaces rendered through one engine using an archetype plus record model.
It separates composition and slot structure from tokens, materials, density, and interaction-state design.
It defines a shared frame of header, body zones, and integrated hatch/V foot for all FACE-1 surfaces.
The six surfaces are channel-mesh, session-drill/session-card, timeline/lanes, board-view, transcript-viz plus search, and embedded-CLI.
Each surface entry includes an archetype, slots, data source expectations, and responsive behavior.
Data sources named include fork read-API, recollection recall/search, transcript index, board JSON, and CLI stream.
The through-line describes navigation and relationship flow among the surfaces.
The document explicitly warns not to pre-skin the surfaces before the DNA direction and keystone-lock land.
standouts: The document treats UI surfaces as archetype records for a single renderer rather than separate bespoke renderers.
It uses spatial metaphors such as mesh, lanes, zones, map, and framed terminal to define surface structure.
It makes visual design an explicit deferred layer while still recording structural and data-binding intent.
conventions: Uses numbered sections for each FACE-1 surface.
Uses repeated bullets for Archetype, Slots, Data, Visual signals, and Responsive, though not every section has every bullet.
Uses emphatic uppercase terms to mark system concepts such as ONE, FACE-1, COMPOSITION/SLOTS, VISUAL TREATMENT, and DEFERRED.
Uses inline code formatting for organism and data identifiers.
Keeps the file as prose design structure rather than executable code or schema.
concerns: Several referenced concepts are not defined in this file, including the projection resolver, keystone-lock, DNA direction, fork read-API, and recollection interfaces.
Some implementation decisions remain provisional inside the file, such as the timeline lane organism being likely new and lit-on-dark command register being a candidate.
The boundary between deferred visual treatment and structural visual signals is repeated and may need a later schema distinction.
notes: Brief this as the FACE-1 structural archetype ledger: six surfaces, their slots, data bindings, and responsive intent, with skinning deferred.
questions: What exact record schema will each archetype receive from the read-API and recollection layers?
Which parts of the listed visual signals are structural requirements versus deferred skin decisions?
What is the formal slot-map shape expected in layouts.json?
How is the projection resolver expected to compute layout across the device axis?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it defines render-independent FACE-1 composition, slots, data bindings, organisms, and responsive intent while explicitly deferring visual treatment.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to preserve the structural contract for FACE-1 so implementation can proceed once data APIs, rendering engine, and visual DNA decisions are available.
novelty: It describes product surfaces as composable archetype substrates with slots and organisms, explicitly separating skeleton, data, and skin.
contribution: Contributes a neutral structural map for FACE-1 surfaces: shared frame, six surface archetypes, slot expectations, data bindings, responsive behavior, and inter-surface navigation relationships that a fused system could retain as composition guidance.
summary_for_embedding: This is a FACE-1 design document defining six render-independent UI archetypes, their slots, data sources, organisms, responsive behavior, and shared frame for a single archetype rendering engine.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to lock the structural and data-binding layer of FACE-1 while deferring visual treatment and interaction states until the DNA direction and keystone-lock are settled.
